text.NRSV: Paul an apostle—sent neither by human commission nor from human authorities, but through Jesus Christ and God the Father, who raised him from the dead—
text.Rotherham: Paul, an apostle,—not from men, nor through man, but through Jesus Christ, and God the Father who raised him from among the dead,
text.KJV: Paul, an apostle, (not of men, neither by man, but by Jesus Christ, and God the Father, who raised him from the dead;)
text.NABRE: I. Address Chapter 1 Greeting. Paul, an apostle not from human beings nor through a human being but through Jesus Christ and God the Father who raised him from the dead,
text.DRB: Paul an Apostle not of men, neither by man, but by Jesus Christ, and God the Father that raised him from the dead,

text.NRSV: and all the members of God’s family who are with me, To the churches of Galatia:
text.Rotherham: And all the brethren with me;—unto the assemblies of Galatia:
text.KJV: And all the brethren which are with me, unto the churches of Galatia:
text.NABRE: and all the brothers who are with me, to the churches of Galatia:
text.DRB: and all the brethren that are with me; to the churches of Galatia.

text.NRSV: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ,
text.Rotherham: Favour unto you and peace, from God our Father, and Lord Jesus Christ,—
text.KJV: Grace be to you and peace from God the Father, and from our Lord Jesus Christ,
text.NABRE: grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ,
text.DRB: Grace to you and peace from God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ,

text.NRSV: who gave himself for our sins to set us free from the present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father,
text.Rotherham: Who gave himself for our sins, that he might deliver us out of the present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father,—
text.KJV: Who gave himself for our sins, that he might deliver us from this present evil world, according to the will of God and our Father:
text.NABRE: who gave himself for our sins that he might rescue us from the present evil age in accord with the will of our God and Father,
text.DRB: who gave himself for our sins, that he might deliver us from this present wicked world, according to the will of our God and Father:

text.NRSV: to whom be the glory forever and ever. Amen.
text.Rotherham: Unto whom be the glory unto the ages of ages: Amen!
text.KJV: To whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.
text.NABRE: to whom be glory forever and ever. Amen.
text.DRB: to whom is glory for ever and ever. Amen.

text.NRSV: I am astonished that you are so quickly deserting the one who called you in the grace of Christ and are turning to a different gospel—
text.Rotherham: I marvel that, thus quickly, ye are moving away from him that called you in the favour of Christ, unto a different glad-message,—
text.KJV: I marvel that ye are so soon removed from him that called you into the grace of Christ unto another gospel:
text.NABRE: II. Loyalty to the Gospel I am amazed that you are so quickly forsaking the one who called you by [the] grace [of Christ] for a different gospel
text.DRB: I marvel that thus so soon you are transferred from him that called you into the grace of Christ, unto another Gospel:

text.NRSV: not that there is another gospel, but there are some who are confusing you and want to pervert the gospel of Christ.
text.Rotherham: Which is not, another, only there are, some, that are troubling you, and wishing to change the glad-message of the Christ.
text.KJV: Which is not another; but there be some that trouble you, and would pervert the gospel of Christ.
text.NABRE: (not that there is another). But there are some who are disturbing you and wish to pervert the gospel of Christ.
text.DRB: which is not another, unless there be some that trouble you, and will invert the Gospel of Christ.

text.NRSV: But even if we or an angel from heaven should proclaim to you a gospel contrary to what we proclaimed to you, let that one be accursed!
text.Rotherham: But, even if, we, or, a messenger out of heaven, announce a glad-message [unto you] aside from that which we announced unto you, accursed, let him be!
text.KJV: But though we, or an angel from heaven, preach any other gospel unto you than that which we have preached unto you, let him be accursed.
text.NABRE: But even if we or an angel from heaven should preach [to you] a gospel other than the one that we preached to you, let that one be accursed!
text.DRB: But although we, or an Angel from Heaven, evangelize to you beside that which we have evangelized to you, be he anathema.

text.NRSV: As we have said before, so now I repeat, if anyone proclaims to you a gospel contrary to what you received, let that one be accursed!
text.Rotherham: As we have said before, even now, again, I say: If anyone is announcing unto you a glad-message aside from that which ye accepted, accursed, let him be!
text.KJV: As we said before, so say I now again, If any man preach any other gospel unto you than that ye have received, let him be accursed.
text.NABRE: As we have said before, and now I say again, if anyone preaches to you a gospel other than the one that you received, let that one be accursed!
text.DRB: As we have said before, so now I say again: If any evangelize to you, beside that which you have received, be he anathema.

text.NRSV: Am I now seeking human approval, or God’s approval? Or am I trying to please people? If I were still pleasing people, I would not be a servant of Christ.
text.Rotherham: For am I, even now, persuading, men, or God? Or am I seeking to please, men? If I had been still pleasing, men, Christ’s servant, had I not been!
text.KJV: For do I now persuade men, or God? or do I seek to please men? for if I yet pleased men, I should not be the servant of Christ.
text.NABRE: Am I now currying favor with human beings or God? Or am I seeking to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I would not be a slave of Christ.
text.DRB: For do I now use persuasion to men, or to God? Or do I seek to please men? If I yet did please men, I should not be the servant of Christ.

text.NRSV: For I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that the gospel that was proclaimed by me is not of human origin;
text.Rotherham: For I make known unto you, brethren, as to the glad-message which was announced by me, that it is not after man;
text.KJV: But I certify you, brethren, that the gospel which was preached of me is not after man.
text.NABRE: III. Paul’s Defense of His Gospel and His Authority His Call by Christ. Now I want you to know, brothers, that the gospel preached by me is not of human origin.
text.DRB: For I do you to understand, brethren, the Gospel that was evangelized of me, that it is not according to man.

text.NRSV: for I did not receive it from a human source, nor was I taught it, but I received it through a revelation of Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: For neither, from man, did I accept it, nor was taught [it],—but through a revealing of Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: For I neither received it of man, neither was I taught it, but by the revelation of Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: For I did not receive it from a human being, nor was I taught it, but it came through a revelation of Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: For neither did I receive it of man, nor learn it; but by the revelation of Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: You have heard, no doubt, of my earlier life in Judaism. I was violently persecuting the church of God and was trying to destroy it.
text.Rotherham: For ye have heard, as to my own manner of life, at one time, in Judaism, how that, exceedingly, was I persecuting the assembly of God, and laying it waste,
text.KJV: For ye have heard of my conversation in time past in the Jews’ religion, how that beyond measure I persecuted the church of God, and wasted it:
text.NABRE: For you heard of my former way of life in Judaism, how I persecuted the church of God beyond measure and tried to destroy it,
text.DRB: For you have heard my conversation sometime in Judaism, that above measure I persecuted the Church of God, and expugned it,

text.NRSV: I advanced in Judaism beyond many among my people of the same age, for I was far more zealous for the traditions of my ancestors.
text.Rotherham: And was making advancement, in Judaism, above many contemporaries in my nation, being, surpassingly zealous, of my paternal instructions.
text.KJV: And profited in the Jews’ religion above many my equals in mine own nation, being more exceedingly zealous of the traditions of my fathers.
text.NABRE: and progressed in Judaism beyond many of my contemporaries among my race, since I was even more a zealot for my ancestral traditions.
text.DRB: and profited in Judaism above many of mine equals in my nation, being more abundantly an emulator of the traditions of my fathers.

text.NRSV: But when God, who had set me apart before I was born and called me through his grace, was pleased
text.Rotherham: But, when God, who set me apart from my mother’s womb and called me through his favour, was well-pleased
text.KJV: But when it pleased God, who separated me from my mother’s womb, and called me by his grace,
text.NABRE: But when [God], who from my mother’s womb had set me apart and called me through his grace, was pleased
text.DRB: But when it pleased him that separated me from my mother's womb, and called me by his grace, to reveal his Son in me,

text.NRSV: to reveal his Son to me, so that I might proclaim him among the Gentiles, I did not confer with any human being,
text.Rotherham: to reveal his Son in me, that I might announce the glad-message regarding him among the nations, straightway, I conferred not with flesh and blood,
text.KJV: To reveal his Son in me, that I might preach him among the heathen; immediately I conferred not with flesh and blood:
text.NABRE: to reveal his Son to me, so that I might proclaim him to the Gentiles, I did not immediately consult flesh and blood,
text.DRB: that I should evangelize him among the Gentiles, incontinent I condescended not to flesh and blood,

text.NRSV: nor did I go up to Jerusalem to those who were already apostles before me, but I went away at once into Arabia, and afterwards I returned to Damascus.
text.Rotherham: Neither went I up unto Jerusalem unto them who, before me, were apostles,—but I went away into Arabia, and again returned unto Damascus.
text.KJV: Neither went I up to Jerusalem to them which were apostles before me; but I went into Arabia, and returned again unto Damascus.
text.NABRE: nor did I go up to Jerusalem to those who were apostles before me; rather, I went into Arabia and then returned to Damascus.
text.DRB: neither came I to Jerusalem to the Apostles my antecessors: but I went into Arabia, and again I returned to Damascus.

text.NRSV: Then after three years I did go up to Jerusalem to visit Cephas and stayed with him fifteen days;
text.Rotherham: Then, after three years, went I up unto Jerusalem, to become acquainted with Cephas, and tarried with him fifteen days;
text.KJV: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to see Peter, and abode with him fifteen days.
text.NABRE: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to confer with Cephas and remained with him for fifteen days.
text.DRB: Then, after three years I came to Jerusalem to see Peter: and tarried with him fifteen days.

text.NRSV: but I did not see any other apostle except James the Lord’s brother.
text.Rotherham: But, other of the apostles, saw I none, save James the brother of the Lord:—
text.KJV: But other of the apostles saw I none, save James the Lord’s brother.
text.NABRE: But I did not see any other of the apostles, only James the brother of the Lord.
text.DRB: But other of the Apostles saw I none; saving James the brother of our Lord.

text.NRSV: In what I am writing to you, before God, I do not lie!
text.Rotherham: Now, as touching the things which I am writing to you, lo! before God, I am not guilty of falsehood:—
text.KJV: Now the things which I write unto you, behold, before God, I lie not.
text.NABRE: (As to what I am writing to you, behold, before God, I am not lying.)
text.DRB: And the things that I write to you; behold before God, that I lie not.

text.NRSV: Then I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia,
text.Rotherham: After that, I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia,
text.KJV: Afterwards I came into the regions of Syria and Cilicia;
text.NABRE: Then I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
text.DRB: After that, I came into the parts of Syria and Cilicia.

text.NRSV: and I was still unknown by sight to the churches of Judea that are in Christ;
text.Rotherham: And was still unknown, by face, unto the assemblies of Judaea which were in Christ,—
text.KJV: And was unknown by face unto the churches of Judea which were in Christ:
text.NABRE: And I was unknown personally to the churches of Judea that are in Christ;
text.DRB: And I was unknown by sight to the churches of Jewry, that were in Christ:

text.NRSV: they only heard it said, “The one who formerly was persecuting us is now proclaiming the faith he once tried to destroy.”
text.Rotherham: Only they were hearing—He that was persecuting us formerly, now, is announcing the glad-message of the faith which he formerly laid waste;
text.KJV: But they had heard only, That he which persecuted us in times past now preacheth the faith which once he destroyed.
text.NABRE: they only kept hearing that “the one who once was persecuting us is now preaching the faith he once tried to destroy.”
text.DRB: but they had heard only, That he which persecuted us sometime, doth now evangelize the faith which sometime he expugned:

text.NRSV: And they glorified God because of me.
text.Rotherham: And they were glorifying, God, in me.
text.KJV: And they glorified God in me.
text.NABRE: So they glorified God because of me.
text.DRB: and in me they glorified God.

text.NRSV: Then after fourteen years I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas, taking Titus along with me.
text.Rotherham: After that, fourteen years later, I, again, went up unto Jerusalem, with Barnabas, taking with me Titus also;
text.KJV: Then fourteen years after I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas, and took Titus with me also.
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 The Council of Jerusalem. Then after fourteen years I again went up to Jerusalem with Barnabas, taking Titus along also.
text.DRB: Then after fourteen years I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas, taking Titus also with me.

text.NRSV: I went up in response to a revelation. Then I laid before them (though only in a private meeting with the acknowledged leaders) the gospel that I proclaim among the Gentiles, in order to make sure that I was not running, or had not run, in vain.
text.Rotherham: And I went up by revelation, and laid before them the glad-message which I proclaim among the nations; privately, however, to them of repute,—lest, by any means, in vain, I should be running, or had run.
text.KJV: And I went up by revelation, and communicated unto them that gospel which I preach among the Gentiles, but privately to them which were of reputation, lest by any means I should run, or had run, in vain.
text.NABRE: I went up in accord with a revelation, and I presented to them the gospel that I preach to the Gentiles—but privately to those of repute—so that I might not be running, or have run, in vain.
text.DRB: And I went up according to revelation: and conferred with them the Gospel which I preach among the Gentiles, but apart with them that seemed to be something, lest perhaps in vain I should run or had run.

text.NRSV: But even Titus, who was with me, was not compelled to be circumcised, though he was a Greek.
text.Rotherham: But, not even Titus, who was with me, though he was a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised;—
text.KJV: But neither Titus, who was with me, being a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised:
text.NABRE: Moreover, not even Titus, who was with me, although he was a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised,
text.DRB: But neither Titus which was with me, whereas he was a Gentile, was compelled to be circumcised:

text.NRSV: But because of false believers secretly brought in, who slipped in to spy on the freedom we have in Christ Jesus, so that they might enslave us—
text.Rotherham: But, [this was] because of the false brethren secretly introduced,—who, indeed, came in secretly to spy out our freedom, which we have in Christ Jesus, that they might bring us into bondage:—
text.KJV: And that because of false brethren unawares brought in, who came in privily to spy out our liberty which we have in Christ Jesus, that they might bring us into bondage:
text.NABRE: but because of the false brothers secretly brought in, who slipped in to spy on our freedom that we have in Christ Jesus, that they might enslave us—
text.DRB: but because of the false brethren craftily brought in, which craftily came in to espy our liberty that we have in Christ Jesus, that they might bring us into servitude.

text.NRSV: we did not submit to them even for a moment, so that the truth of the gospel might always remain with you.
text.Rotherham: Unto whom, not even for an hour, gave we place by the [required] submission,—in order that, the truth of the glad-message, might still abide with you.
text.KJV: To whom we gave place by subjection, no, not for an hour; that the truth of the gospel might continue with you.
text.NABRE: to them we did not submit even for a moment, so that the truth of the gospel might remain intact for you.
text.DRB: To whom we yielded not subjection no not for an hour, that the truth of the Gospel may remain with you.

text.NRSV: And from those who were supposed to be acknowledged leaders (what they actually were makes no difference to me; God shows no partiality)—those leaders contributed nothing to me.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, from them who were reputed to be something,—whatsoever at one time, they were, maketh no difference to me, God accepteth not a man’s person,—unto me, in fact, they who were of repute added nothing further;
text.KJV: But of these who seemed to be somewhat, (whatsoever they were, it maketh no matter to me: God accepteth no man’s person:) for they who seemed to be somewhat in conference added nothing to me:
text.NABRE: But from those who were reputed to be important (what they once were makes no difference to me; God shows no partiality)—those of repute made me add nothing.
text.DRB: But of them that seemed to be something, (what they were some-time, it is nothing to me. God accepteth not the person of man) for to me, they that seemed to be something, added nothing.

text.NRSV: On the contrary, when they saw that I had been entrusted with the gospel for the uncircumcised, just as Peter had been entrusted with the gospel for the circumcised
text.Rotherham: But, on the contrary,—when they saw that I had been entrusted with the glad-message to the uncircumcision, even as, Peter, [with that] to the circumcision,
text.KJV: But contrariwise, when they saw that the gospel of the uncircumcision was committed unto me, as the gospel of the circumcision was unto Peter;
text.NABRE: On the contrary, when they saw that I had been entrusted with the gospel to the uncircumcised, just as Peter to the circumcised,
text.DRB: But contrarywise when they had seen, that to me was committed the Gospel of the prepuce, as to Peter of the circumcision

text.NRSV: (for he who worked through Peter making him an apostle to the circumcised also worked through me in sending me to the Gentiles),
text.Rotherham: For, he that energised in Peter, into an apostleship to the circumcision, energised also in me, for the nations,—
text.KJV: (For he that wrought effectually in Peter to the apostleship of the circumcision, the same was mighty in me toward the Gentiles:)
text.NABRE: for the one who worked in Peter for an apostolate to the circumcised worked also in me for the Gentiles,
text.DRB: (for he that wrought in Peter to the Apostleship of the circumcision, wrought in me also among the Gentiles)

text.NRSV: and when James and Cephas and John, who were acknowledged pillars, recognized the grace that had been given to me, they gave to Barnabas and me the right hand of fellowship, agreeing that we should go to the Gentiles and they to the circumcised.
text.Rotherham: And when they perceived the favour which had been given unto me, James and Cephas and John, who were reputed to be pillars, gave, the right hand of fellowship, unto me and Barnabas, in order that, we, [should go] unto the nations, and, they, unto the circumcision:—
text.KJV: And when James, Cephas, and John, who seemed to be pillars, perceived the grace that was given unto me, they gave to me and Barnabas the right hands of fellowship; that we should go unto the heathen, and they unto the circumcision.
text.NABRE: and when they recognized the grace bestowed upon me, James and Cephas and John, who were reputed to be pillars, gave me and Barnabas their right hands in partnership, that we should go to the Gentiles and they to the circumcised.
text.DRB: and when they had known the grace that was given me, James and Cephas and John, which seemed to be pillars, gave to me and Barnabas the right hands of society: that we unto the Gentiles, & they unto the circumcision:

text.NRSV: They asked only one thing, that we remember the poor, which was actually what I was eager to do.
text.Rotherham: Only that we should remember, the destitute,—as to which I had given diligence, this very thing, to do.
text.KJV: Only they would that we should remember the poor; the same which I also was forward to do.
text.NABRE: Only, we were to be mindful of the poor, which is the very thing I was eager to do. Peter’s Inconsistency at Antioch.
text.DRB: only that we should be mindful of the poor: the which same thing also I was careful to do.

text.NRSV: But when Cephas came to Antioch, I opposed him to his face, because he stood self-condemned;
text.Rotherham: But, when Cephas came unto Antioch, to the face, [even], him, I resisted, because he stood condemned;
text.KJV: But when Peter was come to Antioch, I withstood him to the face, because he was to be blamed.
text.NABRE: And when Cephas came to Antioch, I opposed him to his face because he clearly was wrong.
text.DRB: And when Cephas was come to Antioch, I resisted him in face, because he was reprehensible.

text.NRSV: for until certain people came from James, he used to eat with the Gentiles. But after they came, he drew back and kept himself separate for fear of the circumcision faction.
text.Rotherham: For, before that certain came from James, with them of the nations, used he to eat; whereas, when they came, he used to withdraw, and keep himself separate, fearing them of the circumcision;
text.KJV: For before that certain came from James, he did eat with the Gentiles: but when they were come, he withdrew and separated himself, fearing them which were of the circumcision.
text.NABRE: For, until some people came from James, he used to eat with the Gentiles; but when they came, he began to draw back and separated himself, because he was afraid of the circumcised.
text.DRB: For before that certain came from James, he did eat with the Gentiles: but when they were come, he withdrew and separated himself, fearing them that were of the circumcision.

text.NRSV: And the other Jews joined him in this hypocrisy, so that even Barnabas was led astray by their hypocrisy.
text.Rotherham: And the rest of the Jews [also] used hypocrisy with him, so that, even Barnabas, was carried away by their hypocrisy.
text.KJV: And the other Jews dissembled likewise with him; insomuch that Barnabas also was carried away with their dissimulation.
text.NABRE: And the rest of the Jews [also] acted hypocritically along with him, with the result that even Barnabas was carried away by their hypocrisy.
text.DRB: And to his simulation consented the rest of the Jews, so that Barnabas also was led of them into that simulation.

text.NRSV: But when I saw that they were not acting consistently with the truth of the gospel, I said to Cephas before them all, “If you, though a Jew, live like a Gentile and not like a Jew, how can you compel the Gentiles to live like Jews?”
text.Rotherham: But, when I saw that they were not walking with straightforwardness as regardeth the truth of the glad-message, I said unto Cephas, before all: If, thou, although, a Jew, like them of the nations, and not like the Jews, dost live, how dost thou compel, them of the nations, to live like Jews?
text.KJV: But when I saw that they walked not uprightly according to the truth of the gospel, I said unto Peter before them all, If thou, being a Jew, livest after the manner of Gentiles, and not as do the Jews, why compellest thou the Gentiles to live as do the Jews?
text.NABRE: But when I saw that they were not on the right road in line with the truth of the gospel, I said to Cephas in front of all, “If you, though a Jew, are living like a Gentile and not like a Jew, how can you compel the Gentiles to live like Jews?” Faith and Works.
text.DRB: But when I saw that they walked not rightly to the verity of the Gospel, I said to Cephas before them all: If thou being a Jew, livest Gentile-like and not Judaically, how doest thou compel the Gentiles to Judaize?

text.NRSV: We ourselves are Jews by birth and not Gentile sinners;
text.Rotherham: We, by nature Jews, and not sinners from among the nations,
text.KJV: We who are Jews by nature, and not sinners of the Gentiles,
text.NABRE: We, who are Jews by nature and not sinners from among the Gentiles,
text.DRB: We are by nature Jews, and not of the Gentiles, sinners.

text.NRSV: yet we know that a person is justified not by the works of the law but through faith in Jesus Christ. And we have come to believe in Christ Jesus, so that we might be justified by faith in Christ, and not by doing the works of the law, because no one will be justified by the works of the law.
text.Rotherham: Knowing, however, that a man is not declared righteous by works of law, [nor at all] save through faith in Christ Jesus; even we, on Christ Jesus, believed, that we might be declared righteous—by faith in Christ, and not by works of law; because, by works of law, shall no flesh be declared righteous.
text.KJV: Knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the law, but by the faith of Jesus Christ, even we have believed in Jesus Christ, that we might be justified by the faith of Christ, and not by the works of the law: for by the works of the law shall no flesh be justified.
text.NABRE: [yet] who know that a person is not justified by works of the law but through faith in Jesus Christ, even we have believed in Christ Jesus that we may be justified by faith in Christ and not by works of the law, because by works of the law no one will be justified.
text.DRB: But knowing that man is not justified by the works of the Law, but by the faith of Jesus Christ; we also believe in Christ Jesus, that we may be justified by the faith of Christ, and not by the works of the Law: for the which cause, by the works of the Law no flesh shall be justified.

text.NRSV: But if, in our effort to be justified in Christ, we ourselves have been found to be sinners, is Christ then a servant of sin? Certainly not!
text.Rotherham: Now, if in seeking to be declared righteous in Christ we, ourselves also, were found sinners,—is Christ, therefore, a minister, of sin? Far be it!
text.KJV: But if, while we seek to be justified by Christ, we ourselves also are found sinners, is therefore Christ the minister of sin? God forbid.
text.NABRE: But if, in seeking to be justified in Christ, we ourselves are found to be sinners, is Christ then a minister of sin? Of course not!
text.DRB: But if seeking to be justified in Christ, ourselves also be found sinners; is Christ then a minister of sin? God forbid.

text.NRSV: But if I build up again the very things that I once tore down, then I demonstrate that I am a transgressor.
text.Rotherham: For, if, the things that I pulled down, these, again, I build, a transgressor, I prove, myself, to be.
text.KJV: For if I build again the things which I destroyed, I make myself a transgressor.
text.NABRE: But if I am building up again those things that I tore down, then I show myself to be a transgressor.
text.DRB: For if I build the same things again which I have destroyed, I make myself a prevaricator.

text.NRSV: For through the law I died to the law, so that I might live to God. I have been crucified with Christ;
text.Rotherham: For, I, through means of law, unto law, died, that, unto God, I might live:—
text.KJV: For I through the law am dead to the law, that I might live unto God.
text.NABRE: For through the law I died to the law, that I might live for God. I have been crucified with Christ;
text.DRB: For I by the Law, am dead to the Law, that I may live to God: with Christ I am nailed to the cross.

text.NRSV: and it is no longer I who live, but it is Christ who lives in me. And the life I now live in the flesh I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave himself for me.
text.Rotherham: With Christ, have I been crucified; and, living no longer, am, I, but, living in me, is, Christ,—while, so far as I now do live in flesh, by faith, I live—The faith in the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself up in my behalf.
text.KJV: I am crucified with Christ: nevertheless I live; yet not I, but Christ liveth in me: and the life which I now live in the flesh I live by the faith of the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself for me.
text.NABRE: yet I live, no longer I, but Christ lives in me; insofar as I now live in the flesh, I live by faith in the Son of God who has loved me and given himself up for me.
text.DRB: And I live, now not I; but Christ liveth in me. And that that I live now in the flesh, I live in the faith of the Son of God, who loved me, and delivered himself for me.

text.NRSV: I do not nullify the grace of God; for if justification comes through the law, then Christ died for nothing.
text.Rotherham: I do not set aside the favour of God; for, if, through law, is righteousness, then, Christ, without cause, died.
text.KJV: I do not frustrate the grace of God: for if righteousness come by the law, then Christ is dead in vain.
text.NABRE: I do not nullify the grace of God; for if justification comes through the law, then Christ died for nothing.
text.DRB: I cast not away the grace of God. For if justice be by the Law, then Christ died in vain.

text.NRSV: You foolish Galatians! Who has bewitched you? It was before your eyes that Jesus Christ was publicly exhibited as crucified!
text.Rotherham: O thoughtless Galatians! who hath bewitched you,—before whose very eyes, Jesus Christ, was openly set forth as a crucified one?
text.KJV: O foolish Galatians, who hath bewitched you, that ye should not obey the truth, before whose eyes Jesus Christ hath been evidently set forth, crucified among you?
text.NABRE: IV. Faith and Liberty Chapter 3 Justification by Faith. O stupid Galatians! Who has bewitched you, before whose eyes Jesus Christ was publicly portrayed as crucified?
text.DRB: O senseless Galatians, who hath bewitched you, not to obey the truth, before whose eyes Jesus Christ was proscribed, being crucified among you?

text.NRSV: The only thing I want to learn from you is this: Did you receive the Spirit by doing the works of the law or by believing what you heard?
text.Rotherham: This only, am I wishing to learn from you:—by works of law, received ye, the Spirit? or by a believed report?
text.KJV: This only would I learn of you, Received ye the Spirit by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?
text.NABRE: I want to learn only this from you: did you receive the Spirit from works of the law, or from faith in what you heard?
text.DRB: This only I would learn of you: By the works of the Law, did you receive the Spirit, or by the hearing of the faith?

text.NRSV: Are you so foolish? Having started with the Spirit, are you now ending with the flesh?
text.Rotherham: So thoughtless, are ye? Having made a beginning in Spirit, are ye, now, in flesh, to be made complete?
text.KJV: Are ye so foolish? having begun in the Spirit, are ye now made perfect by the flesh?
text.NABRE: Are you so stupid? After beginning with the Spirit, are you now ending with the flesh?
text.DRB: Are you so foolish, that whereas you began with the spirit, now you will be consummate with the flesh?

text.NRSV: Did you experience so much for nothing?—if it really was for nothing.
text.Rotherham: Such things, suffered ye, in vain,—if at least [it is] even in vain?
text.KJV: Have ye suffered so many things in vain? if it be yet in vain.
text.NABRE: Did you experience so many things in vain?—if indeed it was in vain.
text.DRB: Have you suffered so great things without cause? if yet without cause.

text.NRSV: Well then, does God supply you with the Spirit and work miracles among you by your doing the works of the law, or by your believing what you heard?
text.Rotherham: He then who was supplying unto you the Spirit, and energising mighty works among you, by works of law, or by a believed report [did he it] ?
text.KJV: He therefore that ministereth to you the Spirit, and worketh miracles among you, doeth he it by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?
text.NABRE: Does, then, the one who supplies the Spirit to you and works mighty deeds among you do so from works of the law or from faith in what you heard?
text.DRB: He therefore that giveth you the Spirit, and worketh miracles among you; by the works of the Law, or by the hearing of the faith doeth he it?

text.NRSV: Just as Abraham “believed God, and it was reckoned to him as righteousness,”
text.Rotherham: Even as Abraham—believed in God, and it was reckoned unto him as righteousness.
text.KJV: Even as Abraham believed God, and it was accounted to him for righteousness.
text.NABRE: Thus Abraham “believed God, and it was credited to him as righteousness.”
text.DRB: As, Abraham believed God, and it was reputed to him unto justice.

text.NRSV: so, you see, those who believe are the descendants of Abraham.
text.Rotherham: Be taking note, therefore—that, they who are of faith, the same, are, sons, of Abraham;
text.KJV: Know ye therefore that they which are of faith, the same are the children of Abraham.
text.NABRE: Realize then that it is those who have faith who are children of Abraham.
text.DRB: Know ye therefore that they that are of faith, the same are the children of Abraham.

text.NRSV: And the scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the Gentiles by faith, declared the gospel beforehand to Abraham, saying, “All the Gentiles shall be blessed in you.”
text.Rotherham: And, the scripture, foreseeing, that, by faith, God would declare the nations righteous, fore-announced the good news unto Abraham, saying, All the nations shall be blessed in thee.
text.KJV: And the scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the heathen through faith, preached before the gospel unto Abraham, saying, In thee shall all nations be blessed.
text.NABRE: Scripture, which saw in advance that God would justify the Gentiles by faith, foretold the good news to Abraham, saying, “Through you shall all the nations be blessed.”
text.DRB: And the Scripture foreseeing that God justifieth the Gentiles by faith, shewed unto Abraham before, That in thee shall all nations be blessed.

text.NRSV: For this reason, those who believe are blessed with Abraham who believed.
text.Rotherham: So then, they who are of faith, are blessed with believing Abraham;
text.KJV: So then they which be of faith are blessed with faithful Abraham.
text.NABRE: Consequently, those who have faith are blessed along with Abraham who had faith.
text.DRB: Therefore they that are of faith, shall be blessed with the faithful Abraham.

text.NRSV: For all who rely on the works of the law are under a curse; for it is written, “Cursed is everyone who does not observe and obey all the things written in the book of the law.”
text.Rotherham: For, as many as are of works of law, are, under a curse,—for it is written—Accursed, is everyone that continueth not in all things that are written in the book of the law to do them.
text.KJV: For as many as are of the works of the law are under the curse: for it is written, Cursed is every one that continueth not in all things which are written in the book of the law to do them.
text.NABRE: For all who depend on works of the law are under a curse; for it is written, “Cursed be everyone who does not persevere in doing all the things written in the book of the law.”
text.DRB: For whosoever are of the works of the Law, are under curse. For it is written: Cursed be every one that abideth not in all things that be written in the book of the Law, to do them.

text.NRSV: Now it is evident that no one is justified before God by the law; for “The one who is righteous will live by faith.”
text.Rotherham: Moreover, that, in law, no one, is declared righteous with God, [is] evident,—because, The righteous one, by faith, shall live;
text.KJV: But that no man is justified by the law in the sight of God, it is evident: for, The just shall live by faith.
text.NABRE: And that no one is justified before God by the law is clear, for “the one who is righteous by faith will live.”
text.DRB: But that in the Law no man is justified with God, it is manifest, because the just liveth by faith.

text.NRSV: But the law does not rest on faith; on the contrary, “Whoever does the works of the law will live by them.”
text.Rotherham: And, the law, is not of faith, but—he that hath done them, shall live in them,
text.KJV: And the law is not of faith: but, The man that doeth them shall live in them.
text.NABRE: But the law does not depend on faith; rather, “the one who does these things will live by them.”
text.DRB: But the Law is not by faith: but, He that doeth those things, shall live in them.

text.NRSV: Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law by becoming a curse for us—for it is written, “Cursed is everyone who hangs on a tree”—
text.Rotherham: Christ, hath redeemed, us, out of the curse of the law, having become, in our behalf, a curse;—because it is written—Cursed, is every one that hangeth upon a tree;—
text.KJV: Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the law, being made a curse for us: for it is written, Cursed is every one that hangeth on a tree:
text.NABRE: Christ ransomed us from the curse of the law by becoming a curse for us, for it is written, “Cursed be everyone who hangs on a tree,”
text.DRB: Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the Law, being made a curse for us (because it is written: Cursed is every one that hangeth on a tree)

text.NRSV: in order that in Christ Jesus the blessing of Abraham might come to the Gentiles, so that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.
text.Rotherham: In order that, unto the nations, the blessing of Abraham, might come about in Jesus Christ,—in order that, the promise of the Spirit, we might receive through means of the faith.
text.KJV: That the blessing of Abraham might come on the Gentiles through Jesus Christ; that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.
text.NABRE: that the blessing of Abraham might be extended to the Gentiles through Christ Jesus, so that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.
text.DRB: that on the Gentiles the blessing of Abraham might be made in Christ Jesus: that we may receive the promise of the Spirit by faith.

text.NRSV: Brothers and sisters, I give an example from daily life: once a person’s will has been ratified, no one adds to it or annuls it.
text.Rotherham: Brethren! in human fashion, am I speaking: yet still, a man’s confirmed covenant, no one setteth aside or addeth unto:
text.KJV: Brethren, I speak after the manner of men; Though it be but a man’s covenant, yet if it be confirmed, no man disannulleth, or addeth thereto.
text.NABRE: The Law Did Not Nullify the Promise. Brothers, in human terms I say that no one can annul or amend even a human will once ratified.
text.DRB: Brethren (I speak according to man) yet a man's testament being confirmed no man despiseth, or further disposeth.

text.NRSV: Now the promises were made to Abraham and to his offspring; it does not say, “And to offsprings,” as of many; but it says, “And to your offspring,” that is, to one person, who is Christ.
text.Rotherham: Now, unto Abraham, were spoken the promises—and unto his seed;—He saith not—and unto thy seeds, as of many, but, as of one—and unto thy seed, which is Christ:
text.KJV: Now to Abraham and his seed were the promises made. He saith not, And to seeds, as of many; but as of one, And to thy seed, which is Christ.
text.NABRE: Now the promises were made to Abraham and to his descendant. It does not say, “And to descendants,” as referring to many, but as referring to one, “And to your descendant,” who is Christ.
text.DRB: To Abraham were the promises said, and to his seed. He saith not, And to seeds, as in many: but as in one, And to thy seed, which is Christ.

text.NRSV: My point is this: the law, which came four hundred thirty years later, does not annul a covenant previously ratified by God, so as to nullify the promise.
text.Rotherham: And, this, I say—a covenant previously confirmed by God, the law which, after four hundred and thirty years, hath been brought into being, doth not annul, so as to do away with the promise.
text.KJV: And this I say, that the covenant, that was confirmed before of God in Christ, the law, which was four hundred and thirty years after, cannot disannul, that it should make the promise of none effect.
text.NABRE: This is what I mean: the law, which came four hundred and thirty years afterward, does not annul a covenant previously ratified by God, so as to cancel the promise.
text.DRB: And this I say, the testament being confirmed of God, the Law which was made after four hundred and thirty years, maketh not void to frustrate the promise.

text.NRSV: For if the inheritance comes from the law, it no longer comes from the promise; but God gave it to Abraham through a promise.
text.Rotherham: For, if, by law, is the inheritance, it is, no longer, by promise; but, unto Abraham, through promise, hath God favoured it.
text.KJV: For if the inheritance be of the law, it is no more of promise: but God gave it to Abraham by promise.
text.NABRE: For if the inheritance comes from the law, it is no longer from a promise; but God bestowed it on Abraham through a promise.
text.DRB: For if the inheritance be of the Law, now not of promise. But God gave it to Abraham by promise.

text.NRSV: Why then the law? It was added because of transgressions, until the offspring would come to whom the promise had been made; and it was ordained through angels by a mediator.
text.Rotherham: Why, then, the law? Because of the transgressions, it was added, until such time as the seed should come, unto whom the promise had been made, and was given in charge through messengers, at the hand of a mediator;
text.KJV: Wherefore then serveth the law? It was added because of transgressions, till the seed should come to whom the promise was made; and it was ordained by angels in the hand of a mediator.
text.NABRE: Why, then, the law? It was added for transgressions, until the descendant came to whom the promise had been made; it was promulgated by angels at the hand of a mediator.
text.DRB: Why was the Law then? It was put for transgressions, until the seed came to whom he had promised: ordained by Angels in the hand of a mediator.

text.NRSV: Now a mediator involves more than one party; but God is one.
text.Rotherham: Now, a mediator, is not [a mediator] of one, but, God, is, one.
text.KJV: Now a mediator is not a mediator of one, but God is one.
text.NABRE: Now there is no mediator when only one party is involved, and God is one.
text.DRB: And a mediator is not of one: but God is one.

text.NRSV: Is the law then opposed to the promises of God? Certainly not! For if a law had been given that could make alive, then righteousness would indeed come through the law.
text.Rotherham: Is, the law, then, against the promises [of God]? Far be it! For, if a law had been given, which had been able to give life, verily, in law, would have been our righteousness;
text.KJV: Is the law then against the promises of God? God forbid: for if there had been a law given which could have given life, verily righteousness should have been by the law.
text.NABRE: Is the law then opposed to the promises [of God]? Of course not! For if a law had been given that could bring life, then righteousness would in reality come from the law.
text.DRB: Was the Law then against the promises of God? God forbid. For if there had been a Law given that could justify, undoubtedly justice should be of the Law.

text.NRSV: But the scripture has imprisoned all things under the power of sin, so that what was promised through faith in Jesus Christ might be given to those who believe.
text.Rotherham: But the Scripture, did shut up, all things, under sin, that, the promise by faith in Jesus Christ, might be given to them who have faith,
text.KJV: But the scripture hath concluded all under sin, that the promise by faith of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believe.
text.NABRE: But scripture confined all things under the power of sin, that through faith in Jesus Christ the promise might be given to those who believe. What Faith Has Brought Us.
text.DRB: But the Scripture hath concluded all things under sin: that the promise by the faith of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believe.

text.NRSV: Now before faith came, we were imprisoned and guarded under the law until faith would be revealed.
text.Rotherham: Before the coming of the faith, however, under law, were we being kept in ward, being shut up unto the faith which should afterwards, be revealed.
text.KJV: But before faith came, we were kept under the law, shut up unto the faith which should afterwards be revealed.
text.NABRE: Before faith came, we were held in custody under law, confined for the faith that was to be revealed.
text.DRB: But before the faith came, under the Law we were kept shut up, unto that faith which was to be revealed.

text.NRSV: Therefore the law was our disciplinarian until Christ came, so that we might be justified by faith.
text.Rotherham: So that, the law, hath proved, our tutor, training us, for Christ, in order that, by faith, we might be declared righteous;
text.KJV: Wherefore the law was our schoolmaster to bring us unto Christ, that we might be justified by faith.
text.NABRE: Consequently, the law was our disciplinarian for Christ, that we might be justified by faith.
text.DRB: Therefore the Law was our Pedagogue in Christ: that we may be justified by faith.

text.NRSV: But now that faith has come, we are no longer subject to a disciplinarian,
text.Rotherham: But, the faith having come, no longer, are we, under a tutor;—
text.KJV: But after that faith is come, we are no longer under a schoolmaster.
text.NABRE: But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a disciplinarian.
text.DRB: But when the faith came, now we are not under a pedagogue.

text.NRSV: for in Christ Jesus you are all children of God through faith.
text.Rotherham: For ye, all, are, sons of God, through the faith in Christ Jesus;
text.KJV: For ye are all the children of God by faith in Christ Jesus.
text.NABRE: For through faith you are all children of God in Christ Jesus.
text.DRB: For you are all the children of God by faith in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: As many of you as were baptized into Christ have clothed yourselves with Christ.
text.Rotherham: For ye, as many as, into Christ, have been immersed, have put Christ, on:
text.KJV: For as many of you as have been baptized into Christ have put on Christ.
text.NABRE: For all of you who were baptized into Christ have clothed yourselves with Christ.
text.DRB: For as many of you as are baptized in Christ, have put on Christ.

text.NRSV: There is no longer Jew or Greek, there is no longer slave or free, there is no longer male and female; for all of you are one in Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: There cannot be Jew or Greek, there cannot be bond or free, there cannot be male and female, for, all ye, are one, in Christ Jesus:
text.KJV: There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither bond nor free, there is neither male nor female: for ye are all one in Christ Jesus.
text.NABRE: There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither slave nor free person, there is not male and female; for you are all one in Christ Jesus.
text.DRB: There is not Jew nor Greek, there is not bond nor free, there is not male nor female. For all you are one in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: And if you belong to Christ, then you are Abraham’s offspring, heirs according to the promise.
text.Rotherham: Now, if, ye, are of Christ, by consequence ye are, Abraham’s seed, according to promise, heirs.
text.KJV: And if ye be Christ’s, then are ye Abraham’s seed, and heirs according to the promise.
text.NABRE: And if you belong to Christ, then you are Abraham’s descendant, heirs according to the promise.
text.DRB: And if you be Christ's, then are you the seed of Abraham, heirs according to promise.

text.NRSV: My point is this: heirs, as long as they are minors, are no better than slaves, though they are the owners of all the property;
text.Rotherham: But I say:—for as long a time as, the heir, is an infant, he differeth, nothing, from a servant, though, lord of all,
text.KJV: Now I say, That the heir, as long as he is a child, differeth nothing from a servant, though he be lord of all;
text.NABRE: Chapter 4 God’s Free Children in Christ. I mean that as long as the heir is not of age, he is no different from a slave, although he is the owner of everything,
text.DRB: And I say, as long as the heir is a little one, he differeth nothing from a servant, although he be lord of all,

text.NRSV: but they remain under guardians and trustees until the date set by the father.
text.Rotherham: But is, under guardians, and stewards, until the [day] fore-appointed of the father:
text.KJV: But is under tutors and governors until the time appointed of the father.
text.NABRE: but he is under the supervision of guardians and administrators until the date set by his father.
text.DRB: But is under tutors and governors until the time limited of the father:

text.NRSV: So with us; while we were minors, we were enslaved to the elemental spirits of the world.
text.Rotherham: So also, we, when we were infants, under the elementary principles of the world, were held in servitude;
text.KJV: Even so we, when we were children, were in bondage under the elements of the world:
text.NABRE: In the same way we also, when we were not of age, were enslaved to the elemental powers of the world.
text.DRB: so we also, when we were little ones, were serving under the elements of the world.

text.NRSV: But when the fullness of time had come, God sent his Son, born of a woman, born under the law,
text.Rotherham: But, when the fulness of the time came, God sent forth his Son, who came to be of a woman, who came to be under law,—
text.KJV: But when the fulness of the time was come, God sent forth his Son, made of a woman, made under the law,
text.NABRE: But when the fullness of time had come, God sent his Son, born of a woman, born under the law,
text.DRB: But when the fullness of time came, God sent his Son made of a woman, made under the Law:

text.NRSV: in order to redeem those who were under the law, so that we might receive adoption as children.
text.Rotherham: That, them who were under law, he might redeem, that, the sonship, we might duly receive;—
text.KJV: To redeem them that were under the law, that we might receive the adoption of sons.
text.NABRE: to ransom those under the law, so that we might receive adoption.
text.DRB: that he might redeem them that were under the Law; that we might receive the adoption of sons.

text.NRSV: And because you are children, God has sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, crying, “Abba! Father!”
text.Rotherham: And, because ye are sons, God hath sent forth the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, exclaiming, Abba! Oh Father!
text.KJV: And because ye are sons, God hath sent forth the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying, Abba, Father.
text.NABRE: As proof that you are children, God sent the spirit of his Son into our hearts, crying out, “Abba, Father!”
text.DRB: And because you are sons, God hath sent the Spirit of his son into your hearts crying: Abba, Father.

text.NRSV: So you are no longer a slave but a child, and if a child then also an heir, through God.
text.Rotherham: So that, no longer, art thou a servant, but a son; and, if a son, an heir also, through God.
text.KJV: Wherefore thou art no more a servant, but a son; and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ.
text.NABRE: So you are no longer a slave but a child, and if a child then also an heir, through God. Do Not Throw This Freedom Away.
text.DRB: Therefore now he is not a servant, but a son. And if a son, an heir also by God.

text.NRSV: Formerly, when you did not know God, you were enslaved to beings that by nature are not gods.
text.Rotherham: But, at that time—not knowing God, ye were in servitude unto them who, by nature, are not Gods;
text.KJV: Howbeit then, when ye knew not God, ye did service unto them which by nature are no gods.
text.NABRE: At a time when you did not know God, you became slaves to things that by nature are not gods;
text.DRB: But then indeed not knowing God, you served them that by nature are not Gods.

text.NRSV: Now, however, that you have come to know God, or rather to be known by God, how can you turn back again to the weak and beggarly elemental spirits? How can you want to be enslaved to them again?
text.Rotherham: Whereas, now, having acknowledged God,—or rather, having been acknowledged by God, how turn ye back again unto the weak and beggarly elementary principles, unto which, over again, ye are wishing, to come into servitude?
text.KJV: But now, after that ye have known God, or rather are known of God, how turn ye again to the weak and beggarly elements, whereunto ye desire again to be in bondage?
text.NABRE: but now that you have come to know God, or rather to be known by God, how can you turn back again to the weak and destitute elemental powers? Do you want to be slaves to them all over again?
text.DRB: But now when you have known God, or rather are known of God, how turn you again to the weak & poor elements, which you will serve again?

text.NRSV: You are observing special days, and months, and seasons, and years.
text.Rotherham: Days, ye do narrowly observer, and months, and seasons, and years:—
text.KJV: Ye observe days, and months, and times, and years.
text.NABRE: You are observing days, months, seasons, and years.
text.DRB: You observe days, and months, and times, and years.

text.NRSV: I am afraid that my work for you may have been wasted.
text.Rotherham: I am afraid of you—lest by any means, in vain, I should have toiled for you!
text.KJV: I am afraid of you, lest I have bestowed upon you labour in vain.
text.NABRE: I am afraid on your account that perhaps I have labored for you in vain. Appeal to Former Loyalty.
text.DRB: Ifear you, lest perhaps I have laboured in vain among you.

text.NRSV: Friends, I beg you, become as I am, for I also have become as you are. You have done me no wrong.
text.Rotherham: Become ye as, I, because, I also, [was] as, ye,—brethren, I entreat you. Not at all, have ye wronged me.
text.KJV: Brethren, I beseech you, be as I am; for I am as ye are: ye have not injured me at all.
text.NABRE: I implore you, brothers, be as I am, because I have also become as you are. You did me no wrong;
text.DRB: Be ye as I, because I also am as you: Brethren, I beseech you, you have hurt me nothing.

text.NRSV: You know that it was because of a physical infirmity that I first announced the gospel to you;
text.Rotherham: Howbeit ye know that, by reason of a weakness of the flesh, I myself announced the glad-message unto you, formerly;
text.KJV: Ye know how through infirmity of the flesh I preached the gospel unto you at the first.
text.NABRE: you know that it was because of a physical illness that I originally preached the gospel to you,
text.DRB: And you know that by infirmity of the flesh I evangelized to you heretofore:

text.NRSV: though my condition put you to the test, you did not scorn or despise me, but welcomed me as an angel of God, as Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, your trial, in my flesh, ye despised not, neither spat ye [in disgust], but, as a messenger of God, ye welcomed me—as Christ Jesus.
text.KJV: And my temptation which was in my flesh ye despised not, nor rejected; but received me as an angel of God, even as Christ Jesus.
text.NABRE: and you did not show disdain or contempt because of the trial caused you by my physical condition, but rather you received me as an angel of God, as Christ Jesus.
text.DRB: and your tentation in my flesh you despised not, neither rejected, but as and Angel of God you received me, as Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: What has become of the goodwill you felt? For I testify that, had it been possible, you would have torn out your eyes and given them to me.
text.Rotherham: Where, then, is the happiness ye accounted yours? For I bear you witness—that, if possible, your eyes, ye would have dug out, and given unto me.
text.KJV: Where is then the blessedness ye spake of? for I bear you record, that, if it had been possible, ye would have plucked out your own eyes, and have given them to me.
text.NABRE: Where now is that blessedness of yours? Indeed, I can testify to you that, if it had been possible, you would have torn out your eyes and given them to me.
text.DRB: Where is then your blessedness? for I give you testimony that if it could be done, you would have plucked out your eyes and have given them to me.

text.NRSV: Have I now become your enemy by telling you the truth?
text.Rotherham: So then, your enemy, have I become, by dealing truthfully with you?
text.KJV: Am I therefore become your enemy, because I tell you the truth?
text.NABRE: So now have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?
text.DRB: Am I then become your enemy, telling you the truth?

text.NRSV: They make much of you, but for no good purpose; they want to exclude you, so that you may make much of them.
text.Rotherham: They shew a zeal for you, not honourably, but wish, to shut you out, in order that ye may be zealous for, them.
text.KJV: They zealously affect you, but not well; yea, they would exclude you, that ye might affect them.
text.NABRE: They show interest in you, but not in a good way; they want to isolate you, so that you may show interest in them.
text.DRB: They emulate you not well: but they would exclude you, that you might emulate them.

text.NRSV: It is always good to be made much of for a good purpose, and not only when I am present with you.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit it is, honourable, to show zeal in what is honourable at all times, and not only when I am present with you;—
text.KJV: But it is good to be zealously affected always in a good thing, and not only when I am present with you.
text.NABRE: Now it is good to be shown interest for good reason at all times, and not only when I am with you.
text.DRB: But do you emulate the good in good always: and not only when I am present with you.

text.NRSV: My little children, for whom I am again in the pain of childbirth until Christ is formed in you,
text.Rotherham: My dear children! for whom I, again, am in birth-pains, until Christ be formed within you;—
text.KJV: My little children, of whom I travail in birth again until Christ be formed in you,
text.NABRE: My children, for whom I am again in labor until Christ be formed in you!
text.DRB: My little children, whom I travail withal again, until Christ be formed in you.

text.NRSV: I wish I were present with you now and could change my tone, for I am perplexed about you.
text.Rotherham: I could wish, however, to be present with you, even now, and to change my voice,—because I am perplexed regarding you.
text.KJV: I desire to be present with you now, and to change my voice; for I stand in doubt of you.
text.NABRE: I would like to be with you now and to change my tone, for I am perplexed because of you. An Allegory on Christian Freedom.
text.DRB: And I would be with you now and change my voice: because I am confounded in you.

text.NRSV: Tell me, you who desire to be subject to the law, will you not listen to the law?
text.Rotherham: Tell me! ye who, under law, are wishing to be: The law, do ye not hear?
text.KJV: Tell me, ye that desire to be under the law, do ye not hear the law?
text.NABRE: Tell me, you who want to be under the law, do you not listen to the law?
text.DRB: Tell me, you that will be under the law, have you not read the Law?

text.NRSV: For it is written that Abraham had two sons, one by a slave woman and one by a free woman.
text.Rotherham: For it is written, that, Abraham, had two sons—one by the bondmaid, and one by the free woman;
text.KJV: For it is written, that Abraham had two sons, the one by a bondmaid, the other by a freewoman.
text.NABRE: For it is written that Abraham had two sons, one by the slave woman and the other by the freeborn woman.
text.DRB: For it is written that Abraham had two sons: one of the bond-woman, and one of the free-woman.

text.NRSV: One, the child of the slave, was born according to the flesh; the other, the child of the free woman, was born through the promise.
text.Rotherham: But, he that was of the bondmaid, after the flesh, had been born, whereas, he that was of the free woman, through means of a promise.
text.KJV: But he who was of the bondwoman was born after the flesh; but he of the freewoman was by promise.
text.NABRE: The son of the slave woman was born naturally, the son of the freeborn through a promise.
text.DRB: But he that of the bond-woman, was born according to the flesh: and he that of the free-woman, by the promise.

text.NRSV: Now this is an allegory: these women are two covenants. One woman, in fact, is Hagar, from Mount Sinai, bearing children for slavery.
text.Rotherham: Which things, indeed, may bear another meaning; for, the same, are two covenants,—one, indeed, from Mount Sinai, into bondage, bringing forth, the which is Hagar,—
text.KJV: Which things are an allegory: for these are the two covenants; the one from the mount Sinai, which gendereth to bondage, which is Agar.
text.NABRE: Now this is an allegory. These women represent two covenants. One was from Mount Sinai, bearing children for slavery; this is Hagar.
text.DRB: Which things are said by an allegory. For these are the two testaments. The one from mount Sina, gendering unto bondage; which is Agar,

text.NRSV: Now Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia and corresponds to the present Jerusalem, for she is in slavery with her children.
text.Rotherham: And, the Hagar, is Mount Sinai, in Arabia,—she answereth, however, unto the present Jerusalem, for she is in bondage with her children;
text.KJV: For this Agar is mount Sinai in Arabia, and answereth to Jerusalem which now is, and is in bondage with her children.
text.NABRE: Hagar represents Sinai, a mountain in Arabia; it corresponds to the present Jerusalem, for she is in slavery along with her children.
text.DRB: (for Sina is a mountain in Arabia, which hath affinity to that which now is Jerusalem) and serveth with her children.

text.NRSV: But the other woman corresponds to the Jerusalem above; she is free, and she is our mother.
text.Rotherham: But, the Jerusalem above, is free,—the which is our mother;
text.KJV: But Jerusalem which is above is free, which is the mother of us all.
text.NABRE: But the Jerusalem above is freeborn, and she is our mother.
text.DRB: But that Jerusalem which is above, is free; which is our mother.

text.NRSV: For it is written, “Rejoice, you childless one, you who bear no children; burst into song and shout, you who endure no birth pangs; for the children of the desolate woman are more numerous than the children of the one who is married.”
text.Rotherham: For it is written—Be gladdened, O barren one! that wast not giving birth, break forth and shout, thou that wast not in birth-pains,—because, more, are the children of the deserted one, than of her that had the husband.
text.KJV: For it is written, Rejoice, thou barren that bearest not; break forth and cry, thou that travailest not: for the desolate hath many more children than she which hath an husband.
text.NABRE: For it is written: “Rejoice, you barren one who bore no children; break forth and shout, you who were not in labor; for more numerous are the children of the deserted one than of her who has a husband.”
text.DRB: For it is written: Rejoice thou barren, that bearest not: break forth and cry, that travailest not: because many are the children of the desolate, more than of her that hath a husband.

text.NRSV: Now you, my friends, are children of the promise, like Isaac.
text.Rotherham: And, we, brethren, after the manner of Isaac, are children of a promise.
text.KJV: Now we, brethren, as Isaac was, are the children of promise.
text.NABRE: Now you, brothers, like Isaac, are children of the promise.
text.DRB: But we brethren, according to Isaac, are the children of promise.

text.NRSV: But just as at that time the child who was born according to the flesh persecuted the child who was born according to the Spirit, so it is now also.
text.Rotherham: But, just as, then, he that after the manner of the flesh had been born, did persecute him who [had been born] after the manner of the Spirit, thus, also now.
text.KJV: But as then he that was born after the flesh persecuted him that was born after the Spirit, even so it is now.
text.NABRE: But just as then the child of the flesh persecuted the child of the spirit, it is the same now.
text.DRB: But as then he that was born according to the flesh, persecuted him that was after the spirit; so now also.

text.NRSV: But what does the scripture say? “Drive out the slave and her child; for the child of the slave shall not share the inheritance with the child of the free woman.”
text.Rotherham: But, what saith the scripture? Cast out the serving woman and her son; for in nowise shall the son of the serving woman inherit with the son of the free.
text.KJV: Nevertheless what saith the scripture? Cast out the bondwoman and her son: for the son of the bondwoman shall not be heir with the son of the freewoman.
text.NABRE: But what does the scripture say? “Drive out the slave woman and her son! For the son of the slave woman shall not share the inheritance with the son” of the freeborn.
text.DRB: But what saith the Scripture? Cast out the bond-woman and her son. For the son of the bond-woman shall not be heir with the son of the free-woman.

text.NRSV: So then, friends, we are children, not of the slave but of the free woman.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, brethren, we are not children of a serving woman, but of the free:—
text.KJV: So then, brethren, we are not children of the bondwoman, but of the free.
text.NABRE: Therefore, brothers, we are children not of the slave woman but of the freeborn woman.
text.DRB: Therefore, brethren, we are not the children of the bond-woman, but of the free: by the freedom wherewith Christ hath made us free.

text.NRSV: For freedom Christ has set us free. Stand firm, therefore, and do not submit again to a yoke of slavery.
text.Rotherham: With her freedom, Christ hath made you, free. Stand fast, therefore, and do not, again, with a yoke of servitude, be held fast!
text.KJV: Stand fast therefore in the liberty wherewith Christ hath made us free, and be not entangled again with the yoke of bondage.
text.NABRE: V. Exhortation to Christian Living Chapter 5 The Importance of Faith. For freedom Christ set us free; so stand firm and do not submit again to the yoke of slavery.
text.DRB: Stand, and be not holden in again with the yoke of servitude.

text.NRSV: Listen! I, Paul, am telling you that if you let yourselves be circumcised, Christ will be of no benefit to you.
text.Rotherham: See! I, Paul, say unto you—if ye be getting circumcised, Christ will profit you, nothing.
text.KJV: Behold, I Paul say unto you, that if ye be circumcised, Christ shall profit you nothing.
text.NABRE: It is I, Paul, who am telling you that if you have yourselves circumcised, Christ will be of no benefit to you.
text.DRB: Behold I Paul tell you that if you be circumcised, Christ shall profit you nothing.

text.NRSV: Once again I testify to every man who lets himself be circumcised that he is obliged to obey the entire law.
text.Rotherham: Yea, I bear solemn witness again, unto every man getting circumcised,—that he is, a debtor, to do, the whole law.
text.KJV: For I testify again to every man that is circumcised, that he is a debtor to do the whole law.
text.NABRE: Once again I declare to every man who has himself circumcised that he is bound to observe the entire law.
text.DRB: And I testify again to every man circumciding himself, that he is a debter to do the whole Law.

text.NRSV: You who want to be justified by the law have cut yourselves off from Christ; you have fallen away from grace.
text.Rotherham: Ye have been set aside from Christ, ye who, by law, are to be declared righteous,—out of his favour, ye have fallen;
text.KJV: Christ is become of no effect unto you, whosoever of you are justified by the law; ye are fallen from grace.
text.NABRE: You are separated from Christ, you who are trying to be justified by law; you have fallen from grace.
text.DRB: You are evacuated from Christ, that are justified in the Law: you are fallen from grace.

text.NRSV: For through the Spirit, by faith, we eagerly wait for the hope of righteousness.
text.Rotherham: For, we, in Spirit, by faith, for a hope of righteousness, are ardently waiting,—
text.KJV: For we through the Spirit wait for the hope of righteousness by faith.
text.NABRE: For through the Spirit, by faith, we await the hope of righteousness.
text.DRB: For we in spirit, by faith, expect the hope of justice.

text.NRSV: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision counts for anything; the only thing that counts is faith working through love.
text.Rotherham: For, in Christ [Jesus], neither, circumcision, availeth anything, nor uncircumcision, but faith, through love, energising.
text.KJV: For in Jesus Christ neither circumcision availeth any thing, nor uncircumcision; but faith which worketh by love.
text.NABRE: For in Christ Jesus, neither circumcision nor uncircumcision counts for anything, but only faith working through love. Be Not Misled.
text.DRB: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision availeth ought, nor prepuce: but faith that worketh by charity.

text.NRSV: You were running well; who prevented you from obeying the truth?
text.Rotherham: Ye were running well:—who hath hindered you, that, by truth, ye are not to be persuaded?
text.KJV: Ye did run well; who did hinder you that ye should not obey the truth?
text.NABRE: You were running well; who hindered you from following [the] truth?
text.DRB: You ran well, who hath hindered you not to obey the truth?

text.NRSV: Such persuasion does not come from the one who calls you.
text.Rotherham: The persuasion, [is] not of him that calleth you:—
text.KJV: This persuasion cometh not of him that calleth you.
text.NABRE: That enticement does not come from the one who called you.
text.DRB: The persuasion is not of him that calleth you.

text.NRSV: A little yeast leavens the whole batch of dough.
text.Rotherham: A little leaven, leaveneth, the whole lump.
text.KJV: A little leaven leaveneth the whole lump.
text.NABRE: A little yeast leavens the whole batch of dough.
text.DRB: Alittle leaven corrupted the whole paste.

text.NRSV: I am confident about you in the Lord that you will not take any other view, and that the one who is confusing you will pay the penalty, whoever that may be.
text.Rotherham: I, am persuaded regarding you, in the Lord—that, for nothing else, ye will have any regard; but, he that is troubling you, shall bear the sentence,—whosoever he may be.
text.KJV: I have confidence in you through the Lord, that ye will be none otherwise minded: but he that troubleth you shall bear his judgment, whosoever he be.
text.NABRE: I am confident of you in the Lord that you will not take a different view, and that the one who is troubling you will bear the condemnation, whoever he may be.
text.DRB: I have confidence in you in our Lord, that you will be of no other mind: but he that troubleth you, shall bear the judgement, whosoever he be.

text.NRSV: But my friends, why am I still being persecuted if I am still preaching circumcision? In that case the offense of the cross has been removed.
text.Rotherham: I, however, brethren—if, circumcision, I yet proclaim, why am I yet persecuted? After all, the stumbling-block of the cross hath been set aside.
text.KJV: And I, brethren, if I yet preach circumcision, why do I yet suffer persecution? then is the offence of the cross ceased.
text.NABRE: As for me, brothers, if I am still preaching circumcision, why am I still being persecuted? In that case, the stumbling block of the cross has been abolished.
text.DRB: And as for me, brethren, if as yet I preach circumcision, why do I yet suffer persecution? Then is the scandal of the cross evacuated.

text.NRSV: I wish those who unsettle you would castrate themselves!
text.Rotherham: Oh! that they would even leave off in dismay, who are unsettling you!
text.KJV: I would they were even cut off which trouble you.
text.NABRE: Would that those who are upsetting you might also castrate themselves! Freedom for Service.
text.DRB: I would they were also cut off that trouble you.

text.NRSV: For you were called to freedom, brothers and sisters; only do not use your freedom as an opportunity for self-indulgence, but through love become slaves to one another.
text.Rotherham: For, ye, on a footing of freedom, were called, brethren,—only, [turn] not your freedom into an occasion to the flesh, but, by means of your love, be serving one another;
text.KJV: For, brethren, ye have been called unto liberty; only use not liberty for an occasion to the flesh, but by love serve one another.
text.NABRE: For you were called for freedom, brothers. But do not use this freedom as an opportunity for the flesh; rather, serve one another through love.
text.DRB: For you, brethren, are called into liberty: only make not this liberty an occasion to the flesh, but by charity serve one another.

text.NRSV: For the whole law is summed up in a single commandment, “You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”
text.Rotherham: For, the whole law, in one word, is summed up—[namely] in this,—Thou shall love thy neighbour as thyself.
text.KJV: For all the law is fulfilled in one word, even in this; Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.
text.NABRE: For the whole law is fulfilled in one statement, namely, “You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”
text.DRB: For all the Law is fulfilled in one word: Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.

text.NRSV: If, however, you bite and devour one another, take care that you are not consumed by one another.
text.Rotherham: If, however, one another, ye bite and devour, take heed lest, by one another, ye be consumed!
text.KJV: But if ye bite and devour one another, take heed that ye be not consumed one of another.
text.NABRE: But if you go on biting and devouring one another, beware that you are not consumed by one another.
text.DRB: But if you bite and eat one another, take heed you be not consumed one of another.

text.NRSV: Live by the Spirit, I say, and do not gratify the desires of the flesh.
text.Rotherham: I say, moreover—by Spirit, be walking, and, fleshly coveting, ye will in nowise fulfil;
text.KJV: This I say then, Walk in the Spirit, and ye shall not fulfil the lust of the flesh.
text.NABRE: I say, then: live by the Spirit and you will certainly not gratify the desire of the flesh.
text.DRB: And I say, walk in the spirit, and the lusts of the flesh you shall not accomplish.

text.NRSV: For what the flesh desires is opposed to the Spirit, and what the Spirit desires is opposed to the flesh; for these are opposed to each other, to prevent you from doing what you want.
text.Rotherham: For, the flesh, coveted against the Spirit, but, the Spirit, against the flesh,—for, these, unto one another, are opposed, lest, whatsoever things ye chance to desire, these, ye should be doing!
text.KJV: For the flesh lusteth against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh: and these are contrary the one to the other: so that ye cannot do the things that ye would.
text.NABRE: For the flesh has desires against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh; these are opposed to each other, so that you may not do what you want.
text.DRB: For the flesh lusteth against the spirit, and the spirit against the flesh: for these are adversaries one to another: that not what things soever you will, these you do.

text.NRSV: But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not subject to the law.
text.Rotherham: And, if, by Spirit, ye are being led, ye are not under law.
text.KJV: But if ye be led of the Spirit, ye are not under the law.
text.NABRE: But if you are guided by the Spirit, you are not under the law.
text.DRB: But if you be led by the spirit, you are not under the Law.

text.NRSV: Now the works of the flesh are obvious: fornication, impurity, licentiousness,
text.Rotherham: Manifest, however, are the works of the flesh, which, indeed, are—fornication, impurity, wantonness,
text.KJV: Now the works of the flesh are manifest, which are these; Adultery, fornication, uncleanness, lasciviousness,
text.NABRE: Now the works of the flesh are obvious: immorality, impurity, licentiousness,
text.DRB: And the works of the flesh be manifest, which are fornication, uncleanness, impudicity, lechery,

text.NRSV: idolatry, sorcery, enmities, strife, jealousy, anger, quarrels, dissensions, factions,
text.Rotherham: idolatry, enchantment, enmities, strife, jealousy, outbursts of wrath, factions, divisions, parties,
text.KJV: Idolatry, witchcraft, hatred, variance, emulations, wrath, strife, seditions, heresies,
text.NABRE: idolatry, sorcery, hatreds, rivalry, jealousy, outbursts of fury, acts of selfishness, dissensions, factions,
text.DRB: serving of idols, witch-crafts, enmities, contentions, emulations, angers, brawls, dissensions, sects,

text.NRSV: envy, drunkenness, carousing, and things like these. I am warning you, as I warned you before, that those who do such things will not inherit the kingdom of God.
text.Rotherham: envyings, drunkenness, revellings;—and such things as these: as to which I forewarn you, even as I have forewarned you,—that, they who such things as these do practise, shall not inherit, God’s kingdom.
text.KJV: Envyings, murders, drunkenness, revellings, and such like: of the which I tell you before, as I have also told you in time past, that they which do such things shall not inherit the kingdom of God.
text.NABRE: occasions of envy, drinking bouts, orgies, and the like. I warn you, as I warned you before, that those who do such things will not inherit the kingdom of God.
text.DRB: envies, murders, ebrieties, commessations, and such like. Which I foretell you, as I have foretold you, that they which do such things, shall not obtain the Kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: By contrast, the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, generosity, faithfulness,
