text.Rotherham: But, the fruit of the Spirit, is—love, joy, peace, long-suffering, graciousness, goodness, faithfulness,
text.KJV: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith,
text.NABRE: In contrast, the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, generosity, faithfulness,
text.DRB: But the fruit of the Spirit is, Charity, joy, peace, patience, benignity, goodness, longanimity,

text.NRSV: gentleness, and self-control. There is no law against such things.
text.Rotherham: meekness, self-control;—against such things as these, there is no law.
text.KJV: Meekness, temperance: against such there is no law.
text.NABRE: gentleness, self-control. Against such there is no law.
text.DRB: mildness, faith, modesty, continency, chastity. Against such there is no law.

text.NRSV: And those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.
text.Rotherham: And, they who are of Christ Jesus, have crucified, the flesh, with its susceptibilities and covetings.
text.KJV: And they that are Christ’s have crucified the flesh with the affections and lusts.
text.NABRE: Now those who belong to Christ [Jesus] have crucified their flesh with its passions and desires.
text.DRB: And they that be Christ's, have crucified their flesh with the vices and concupiscences.

text.NRSV: If we live by the Spirit, let us also be guided by the Spirit.
text.Rotherham: If we live by Spirit, by Spirit, let us also walk.
text.KJV: If we live in the Spirit, let us also walk in the Spirit.
text.NABRE: If we live in the Spirit, let us also follow the Spirit.
text.DRB: If we live in the spirit, in the spirit also let us walk.

text.NRSV: Let us not become conceited, competing against one another, envying one another.
text.Rotherham: Let us not become vain-glorious,—one another, challenging, one another, envying.
text.KJV: Let us not be desirous of vain glory, provoking one another, envying one another.
text.NABRE: Let us not be conceited, provoking one another, envious of one another.
text.DRB: Let us not be made desirous of vain glory, provoking one another, envying one another.

text.NRSV: My friends, if anyone is detected in a transgression, you who have received the Spirit should restore such a one in a spirit of gentleness. Take care that you yourselves are not tempted.
text.Rotherham: Brethren! if a man should even be overtaken in any fault, ye, the spiritual, be restoring such a one, in a spirit of meekness, looking to thyself, lest, even thou, be put to the test.
text.KJV: Brethren, if a man be overtaken in a fault, ye which are spiritual, restore such an one in the spirit of meekness; considering thyself, lest thou also be tempted.
text.NABRE: Chapter 6 Life in the Community of Christ. Brothers, even if a person is caught in some transgression, you who are spiritual should correct that one in a gentle spirit, looking to yourself, so that you also may not be tempted.
text.DRB: Brethren, and if a man be preoccupated in any fault, you that are spiritual, instruct such an one in the spirit of lenity, considering thine own self, lest thou also be tempted.

text.NRSV: Bear one another’s burdens, and in this way you will fulfill the law of Christ.
text.Rotherham: One another’s burdens, be ye bearing, and, so, fill up the law of the Christ.
text.KJV: Bear ye one another’s burdens, and so fulfil the law of Christ.
text.NABRE: Bear one another’s burdens, and so you will fulfill the law of Christ.
text.DRB: Bear ye one another's burdens: and so you shall fulfill the law of Christ.

text.NRSV: For if those who are nothing think they are something, they deceive themselves.
text.Rotherham: For, if any one thinketh he is something, when he is, nothing, he deceiveth himself;
text.KJV: For if a man think himself to be something, when he is nothing, he deceiveth himself.
text.NABRE: For if anyone thinks he is something when he is nothing, he is deluding himself.
text.DRB: For if any man esteem himself to be something, whereas he is nothing, he seduceth himself.

text.NRSV: All must test their own work; then that work, rather than their neighbor’s work, will become a cause for pride.
text.Rotherham: But, his own work, let each one be putting to the proof, and, then, for himself alone, the matter of boasting, shall he have, and not for some other,
text.KJV: But let every man prove his own work, and then shall he have rejoicing in himself alone, and not in another.
text.NABRE: Each one must examine his own work, and then he will have reason to boast with regard to himself alone, and not with regard to someone else;
text.DRB: But let every one prove his own work, and so in himself only shall he have the glory and not in another.

text.NRSV: For all must carry their own loads.
text.Rotherham: For, each one, his own proper load, shall bear:—
text.KJV: For every man shall bear his own burden.
text.NABRE: for each will bear his own load.
text.DRB: For every one shall bear his own burden.

text.NRSV: Those who are taught the word must share in all good things with their teacher.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, let him who is being orally instructed in the word, have fellowship with him that is so instructing him, in all good things.
text.KJV: Let him that is taught in the word communicate unto him that teacheth in all good things.
text.NABRE: One who is being instructed in the word should share all good things with his instructor.
text.DRB: And let him that is catechized in the word, communicate to him that catechizeth him, in all his goods.

text.NRSV: Do not be deceived; God is not mocked, for you reap whatever you sow.
text.Rotherham: Be not deceiving yourselves! God, is not to be mocked; for, whatsoever a man soweth, the same, shall he also reap,—
text.KJV: Be not deceived; God is not mocked: for whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap.
text.NABRE: Make no mistake: God is not mocked, for a person will reap only what he sows,
text.DRB: Be not deceived, God is not mocked.

text.NRSV: If you sow to your own flesh, you will reap corruption from the flesh; but if you sow to the Spirit, you will reap eternal life from the Spirit.
text.Rotherham: Because, he that soweth into his own flesh, out of the flesh, shall reap corruption, whereas, he that soweth into the Spirit, out of the Spirit, shall reap age-abiding life.
text.KJV: For he that soweth to his flesh shall of the flesh reap corruption; but he that soweth to the Spirit shall of the Spirit reap life everlasting.
text.NABRE: because the one who sows for his flesh will reap corruption from the flesh, but the one who sows for the spirit will reap eternal life from the spirit.
text.DRB: For what things a man shall sow, those also shall he reap. For he that soweth in his flesh, of the flesh also shall reap corruption. But he that soweth in the spirit, of the spirit shall reap life everlasting.

text.NRSV: So let us not grow weary in doing what is right, for we will reap at harvest time, if we do not give up.
text.Rotherham: And, in doing that which is honourable, let us not be fainthearted; for, in due season, we shall reap, if we faint not.
text.KJV: And let us not be weary in well doing: for in due season we shall reap, if we faint not.
text.NABRE: Let us not grow tired of doing good, for in due time we shall reap our harvest, if we do not give up.
text.DRB: And doing good, let us not fail. For in due time we shall reap not failing.

text.NRSV: So then, whenever we have an opportunity, let us work for the good of all, and especially for those of the family of faith.
text.Rotherham: Hence, then, as we have opportunity, let us be working what is good, towards all,—but, especially, towards the family of the faith.
text.KJV: As we have therefore opportunity, let us do good unto all men, especially unto them who are of the household of faith.
text.NABRE: So then, while we have the opportunity, let us do good to all, but especially to those who belong to the family of the faith. VI. Conclusion Final Appeal.
text.DRB: Therefore whiles we have time, let us work good to all, but especially to the domesticals of the faith.

text.NRSV: See what large letters I make when I am writing in my own hand!
text.Rotherham: See! with what large letters, unto you, I have written, with my own hand:—
text.KJV: Ye see how large a letter I have written unto you with mine own hand.
text.NABRE: See with what large letters I am writing to you in my own hand!
text.DRB: See with what manner of letters I have written to you with mine own hand.

text.NRSV: It is those who want to make a good showing in the flesh that try to compel you to be circumcised—only that they may not be persecuted for the cross of Christ.
text.Rotherham: As many as are wishing to make a good show in flesh, the same, are compelling you to get circumcised,—only that, for the cross of Christ [Jesus], they may not be suffering persecution!
text.KJV: As many as desire to make a fair shew in the flesh, they constrain you to be circumcised; only lest they should suffer persecution for the cross of Christ.
text.NABRE: It is those who want to make a good appearance in the flesh who are trying to compel you to have yourselves circumcised, only that they may not be persecuted for the cross of Christ.
text.DRB: Whosoever will please in the flesh, they force you to be circumcised, only that they may not suffer the persecution of the cross of Christ.

text.NRSV: Even the circumcised do not themselves obey the law, but they want you to be circumcised so that they may boast about your flesh.
text.Rotherham: For, not even they who are getting circumcised, are, themselves, observing law,—but are wishing you to be circumcised, that, in your flesh, they may boast themselves.
text.KJV: For neither they themselves who are circumcised keep the law; but desire to have you circumcised, that they may glory in your flesh.
text.NABRE: Not even those having themselves circumcised observe the law themselves; they only want you to be circumcised so that they may boast of your flesh.
text.DRB: For neither they that are circumcised, do keep the Law: but they will have you to be circumcised, that they may glory in your flesh.

text.NRSV: May I never boast of anything except the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.
text.Rotherham: With me, however, far be it! to be boasting, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ,—whereby, unto me, a world hath been crucified, and, I, unto a world;
text.KJV: But God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom the world is crucified unto me, and I unto the world.
text.NABRE: But may I never boast except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.
text.DRB: But God forbid that I should glory, saving in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ; by whom the world is crucified to me, and I to the world.

text.NRSV: For neither circumcision nor uncircumcision is anything; but a new creation is everything!
text.Rotherham: For neither circumcision is anything, nor uncircumcision, but a new creation;—
text.KJV: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision availeth any thing, nor uncircumcision, but a new creature.
text.NABRE: For neither does circumcision mean anything, nor does uncircumcision, but only a new creation.
text.DRB: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision availeth ought, nor prepuce, but a new creature.

text.NRSV: As for those who will follow this rule—peace be upon them, and mercy, and upon the Israel of God.
text.Rotherham: And, as many as, by this rule, shall walk, peace be upon them, and mercy, and upon the Israel of God.
text.KJV: And as many as walk according to this rule, peace be on them, and mercy, and upon the Israel of God.
text.NABRE: Peace and mercy be to all who follow this rule and to the Israel of God.
text.DRB: And whosoever shall follow this rule, peace upon them, and mercy, and upon the Israel of God.

text.NRSV: From now on, let no one make trouble for me; for I carry the marks of Jesus branded on my body.
text.Rotherham: For the rest, annoyances, unto me, let no one be offering; for, I, the brandmarks of Jesus, in my body, am bearing.
text.KJV: From henceforth let no man trouble me: for I bear in my body the marks of the Lord Jesus.
text.NABRE: From now on, let no one make troubles for me; for I bear the marks of Jesus on my body.
text.DRB: From henceforth let no man be troublesome to me. For I bear the marks of our Lord Jesus in my body.

text.NRSV: May the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brothers and sisters. Amen.
text.Rotherham: The favour of our Lord Jesus Christ, be with your spirit, brethren. Amen.
text.KJV: Brethren, the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen. Unto the Galatians written from Rome.
text.NABRE: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brothers. Amen.
text.DRB: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brethren. Amen.
